inference_steps: [425s to 429s; 427s to 467s] The later reviewed play remains anchored around 427s. The text context mentions another reviewed play beginning around 427-467s, and the later chronological frames show the associated play progression at 425s-429s, with the ball landing at 427s.

[358s to 362s; 425s to 429s] Step 1 confirms that the target shot before that reviewed play is the earlier boundary sequence rather than the 427s review sequence itself. Chronologically, the retrieved frames include an earlier run at 358s-362s and a later run at 425s-429s, and the evidence identifies Images 1-5 at 358s-362s as the sequence before the second reviewed play. This supports using the earlier 358s-362s sequence as the target referred to in the question, though the exact shot-boundary wording 'two shots before' is only partially grounded.

[357s to 363s; 359s to 360s] Using only the dense chronological frames around the earlier sequence, the local event order is 357s batsman hits the ball, 358s ball in the air, 359s ball lands near the boundary, 360s ball hits the ground near the MUGHAL STEEL boards, and 361s-363s ball rolls. The spectator/blue-shirted man with a white object is therefore relevant during the ball-near-spectator interval centered on about 359s-360s.

[359s to 363s] The dense frames do not show enough motion to count complete flag-wave cycles. They support only that a blue-shirted man has a white object that could be a flag or cloth; they do not clearly show a full raise-and-return wave, and one dense observation explicitly says he is not clearly waving in Image 4 and seems to be holding it down. Under the counting rule, a held-up flag, a partial raise, or motion continuing off-sequence cannot be counted as a full wave, so the exact count among 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 remains unresolved.

evidence.evidence_text: Retrieved dense chronological frames at 357s, 358s, 359s, 360s, 361s, 362s, and 363s around the earlier boundary sequence.

Grounded review and event context: text context mentions another reviewed play beginning around 427-467s; the later frame sequence at 425s-429s shows batsman hit, ball in air, ball landing, then rolling; the earlier sequence at 358s-362s precedes that later reviewed play. In the earlier sequence, the ball is in the air at 359s, lands near the boundary/stands at 360s, hits the ground near the advertising boards at 361s, and a nearby man in blue is holding a white object that may be a flag or cloth.

Dense chronological inspection of 357s-363s: 357s shows the batsman hitting the ball, 358s the ball in the air, 359s the ball landing near the boundary, 360s the ball hitting the ground near the MUGHAL STEEL boards, and 361s-363s the ball rolling. The same dense inspection notes only a white object near the boundary / possible flag or cloth and does not clearly show a complete waving cycle; in Image 4 the blue-shirted man is not clearly waving and seems to be holding it down.
